name: CFOP 2-Look Last Layer
description: CFOP with 2-Look Last Layer — the most popular intermediate approach. Uses full OLL (57 algorithms) and full PLL (21 algorithms) so each last layer step is solved in a single look and recognition. This is what most cubers mean by 'learning full CFOP'. Combined with efficient F2L, this is enough for sub-20 or even sub-15 averages.

steps.name: Cross
steps.description: Solve 4 bottom-layer edges in under 8 moves, fully planned during the 15-second inspection. Advanced solvers plan the cross and predict the first F2L pair.
tips: Always plan the full cross during inspection
Consider color neutrality for easier crosses
Spot your first F2L pair during inspection

steps.name: F2L (First Two Layers)
steps.description: Solve the first two layers by pairing and inserting corner-edge pairs into 4 slots. Use a mix of intuitive understanding and learned algorithms for the 41 standard cases. Develop lookahead to track the next pair while inserting the current one.
tips: Know optimal solutions for all 41 cases
Have 2-3 solutions per case for angle flexibility
Reduce rotations with wide moves and back-slot insertions
Lookahead is the single most impactful skill to develop

steps.name: OLL (Orientation of Last Layer)
steps.description: Orient all last layer pieces in one algorithm. Full OLL contains 57 distinct cases, each with a unique top-face pattern. Recognition takes practice but becomes instant with drilling.
tips: Learn in groups: dots, P-shapes, W-shapes, L-shapes, lines, crosses
Focus on recognition speed — the alg should be automatic
Some OLLs are rare; prioritize common cases first

steps.name: PLL (Permutation of Last Layer)
steps.description: Permute all last layer pieces in one algorithm. 21 PLL cases, each named after a letter. Two-sided recognition (looking at front + one side) enables fast identification.
tips: Learn two-sided recognition for instant PLL identification
G-perms are the hardest — spend extra time on them
Practice AUF prediction to save 0.3-0.5 seconds per solve

pros: 1-look OLL and PLL — fast last layer
Enough for sub-15 with good F2L
cons: 78 algorithms is a significant memorization effort
Diminishing returns after full OLL/PLL — next improvements come from F2L
Some OLL cases are rare and hard to justify learning
creator: Jessica Fridrich
